learning_outcomes:
RELS 1.  |  Major
programme:
Knowledge: Graduates will
have acquired the following:
(1)  |  The
understanding of religion in its historical settings and contemporary
contexts of modern living, globally and locally.
(2)  |  The
understanding of religion in its different dimensions.
(3)  |  The
understanding of religion through a crtical analysis.
(4)  |  A
basic knowledge of major religious traditions in the world.
Skills: Graduates will
have acquired the following:
(1)  |  Communication
and presentational (oral and written) skills.
(2)  |  Analytical
and problem solving skills.
(3)  |  Social
skills and team-work skills.
(4)  |  Skills
of critical intelligence and appreciative thinking.
(5)  |  Skills
in cross-cultural understanding and intercultural literacy.
(6)  |  Research
skills.
Attitude: Graduates will
have acquired the following:
(1)  |  Awareness
of the plurality and multi-faceted complexity within religious traditions of
the issues of multiple and conflicting interpretations of religious
traditions.
(2)  |  Self-critical
awareness of pre-suppositions and recognition.
(3)  |  A
critical and yet sympathetic understanding and appreciation of religious
traditions especially those to which one does not belong.
(4)  |  Sensitivity
to the ultimate concern and the religious dimension of human life.
2.  |  Minor
programme:
i.  |  Arab
Studies
Upon completion of the programme, students will have
acquired the following learning outcomes in three aspects: I. Knowledge outcomes: (1) Understand religions and cultures of
the Arab World in their historical settings and contemporary contexts of
modern living. (2) Recognize central themes and issues
in the Arab World, especially with respect to cultural and religious
dimensions. II. Skills outcomes: (1) Basic command of the Arabic language. (2) Engage in a critical analysis of
issues pertaining to the Arab World by the methods of the humanities and
social sciences. III. Attitude outcomes: (1) Have awareness of plurality and
multi-faceted complexity within Arab cultural, political and religious
traditions, and of the issues of multiple and conflicting interpretations of
language and symbols, texts and traditions. (2) Acknowledge the contested and
provisional nature of knowledge and understanding. Show tolerance of diverse
positions and have respect for others’ views.
ii.  |  Religious
Studies
Upon
the completion of the Minor programme, students will have demonstrated basic
understanding of fundamental issues related to the subject, acquired
knowledge of some aspects of the subject with sufficient exposure and at a
suitable level to allow further study in this subject or related subjects or
to pursue a related career.
study_scheme:
Religious Studies Religious Studies Applicable to students admitted in 2023-24
Major
Programme Requirement
Students are required to
complete a minimum of 69 units of courses as follows:
Units
1.  |  Faculty Package:  |  9
CURE1110 and any two courses
from:
ANTH1020, ARTS1004, 1005, 1010, BMBL1001,
CHES1100, CHLL1900 or 1902 or 1903, CURE1000, 1400[CUMT1000],
ENGE1000, FAAS1900, HIST1000, 1700 or 1701, JASP1090, LING1000, MUSC1000,
PHIL1110, THEO1000, TRAN1000
2.  |  Required Courses:  |  27
(a)  |  CURE1111,
1122, 1123, 2157, 2170
(b)  |  1
course from the following selected courses from Area 1: CURE2113, 2114,
2115, 3375
(c)  |  1
course from the following selected courses from Area 2: CURE1124, 2161,
2225, 2226, 2227
(d)  |  1
course from the following selected courses from Area 4: CURE2154, 2155,
2180, THEO3215, 3217
(e)  |  1
Capstone course from
the following selected courses from Area 5: CURE4153, 4159
3.  |  Elective Courses:  |  33
(a)  |  9
courses at 2000 or above level from the Religious Studies Areas[a][b]
(b)  |  2
courses from the Cultural Studies Areas [a]
Concentrations
Students
may choose to declare a concentration by taking the following elective
courses [a] in place of 3 “Elective Courses” above:
Religion and
Culture
(a)  |  2 courses from CURE1009, 1010,
2001[1001]*, 2006[1006]*
(b)  |  9 CURE courses at 2000 or
above level, of which at least 2 courses are from the Cultural Studies
Areas and at least 7 courses are from the Religious Studies Areas
Religious
Education
(a)  |  6 units of EDUC courses at
2000 and above level from Educational Core Component offered by the
Faculty of Education
(b)  |  CURE 2142, 2144, 3144
(c)  |  2 courses from CURE2131,
3134, THEO1211, 1212
(d)  |  4 CURE courses at 2000 or
above level from the Religious Studies Areas
Arab Studies
(a)  |  CURE2226, 2228
(b)  |  6 units of language courses: (i) CURE2180 or
ARAB1000; and CURE2181; OR (ii) ARAB2050
(c)  |  4 courses from CURE3114,
3129, 3376, 3378, 4157, 4158
(d)  |  3 CURE courses at 2000 or
above level from the Religious Studies Areas (except CURE2180 and 2181)
Total:  |  69
Explanatory
Notes:
1.  |  ARAB, CURE, EDUC and THEO
courses at 2000 and above level (except those marked with *) as included
in the respective concentrations will be included in the calculation of
Major GPA for honours classification for students specialize in
the relevant concentrations.
2.  |  Courses counted towards the
fulfillment of one of the above listed requirements cannot be used to
fulfill concentration requirements.
[a]  |  Excluding
the courses taken to fulfil requirement 1 and 2 above.
[b]  |  For students
not specialising in any concentration, apart from the THEO courses listed
in the course list, students may take other THEO courses, subject to
a maximum of 5 THEO courses, as their elective courses to fulfill
requirement 3(a).
